title: 101  Prabhupada says we should take some gift while visiting a temple
speaker: HDG Srila Prabhupada

segments.text: Pravupada also mentions that whenever we go and when we go to the temple or we meet,
we are going to meet the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
we should also take something, take some gifts or presents or some offering to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
We should never go empty-handed.
That is why we see generally in all the temples there is a custom that whenever devotees go,
go something, some flower, some fruit.
Krishna is not expecting a big offering from us.
Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita,
You can offer him leaf, water or a fruit.
These things are very easy to get.
We don't need to possess a lot of wealth or money.
It is easily obtainable.
But more than even this,
What?
What Krishna is really looking at is the feeling with which we are offering,
the devotion with which we are offering.
Yome Bhaktaya Prayachati.
A devotee attitude.
An attitude of, yes, he is my Supreme Lord, he is my father.
Just like I would like to offer my parents.
Generally all of us have this.
We offer something very best, something very precious to our parents, right?
And we have no issues about that.
Rather we will be very happy.
We will always be looking for,
how to offer the best,
to my father, to my mother.
Because I exchange a very loving relationship with my father and mother.
So in the same way,
Krishna is our Supreme Father.
And everything that I have got is from my father.
So actually our offering to the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
in the strictest sense,
is not really an offering from us.
It is the Lord's own wealth, the Lord's own profit.
Everything belongs to the Lord.
And I am only offering it back to the Lord.
And the Lord also actually is not in need of any offering, per se.
He is self-sufficient.
He is Swarat.
He doesn't need anything.
He has hundreds and thousands of servants.
And he has all the opulences.
Aishwarya, one of the qualities of the Supreme Personality of God is Aishwarya.
He has enough of everything.
But he is just looking for that love and devotion.
A small little bit of offering,
that we give to him with love and devotion.
He is more eager to take,
he is more eager to take that,
than anything else.
Just like Sudama,
he got chipped rice.
It is considered to be something very insignificant,
for Krishna, who is the King of Dwarka.
But he relished that,
because Sudama brought it with love and devotion.
So this is how we offer to Krishna,
with love and devotion,
whatever best we have, we can offer.
Believe it or not,
no one else's blessings can make that possible.
But here is what happens...
If one asks a question,
we will make it transparent.
He knows.
The silent Woods of night
